name: Autonomous Coding Agent
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Code generation and reasoning engine — task decomposition, code synthesis, bug diagnosis, refactoring plans, test generation, code review, and multi-step agentic reasoning loops with tool-use capabilities

dev.note: ~80K tokens/day, agent loop prototyping and testing

prod.note: ~700K tokens/day, production coding tasks with multi-step reasoning

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for team-wide autonomous coding at scale

services.name: GitHub Actions
services.purpose: Execution sandbox — isolated CI/CD environments for running agent-generated code, executing test suites, performing builds, linting, security scanning, and validating code changes before PR submission

services.name: Azure Container Apps
services.purpose: Agent runtime environment — hosts the autonomous coding agent service, manages agent sessions, provides isolated execution contexts per task, and handles concurrent agent instances with auto-scaling

services.name: Azure Blob Storage
services.purpose: Agent workspace persistence — repository clones, intermediate code artifacts, test results, build outputs, agent session logs, and code diff archives for audit and replay

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Agent state and task management — task queue, agent session state, reasoning trace history, code change records, review feedback, and task completion analytics

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: OpenAI API keys, GitHub tokens (PAT/App), Container Apps secrets, storage keys, and agent authentication certificates

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Agent observability — task completion rate, reasoning loop iterations, token consumption per task, test pass rate, code quality metrics, agent session duration, and error diagnosis tracking

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for routine tasks (formatting, simple refactors, boilerplate generation, test scaffolding) and reserve GPT-4o for complex reasoning (architecture decisions, bug diagnosis, multi-file refactoring) — reduces AI costs by 50-60%
Implement reasoning loop budgets — cap the maximum number of agent iterations per task (e.g., 10 loops) to prevent runaway token consumption on tasks the agent cannot resolve
Cache repository file contents during an agent session — avoid re-reading unchanged files on each reasoning loop iteration, which can account for 30-40% of input tokens
Use GitHub Actions free tier minutes for dev/staging and scale runners only for production workloads — most test validation runs complete in under 5 minutes
Implement task difficulty estimation before dispatching to the agent — route simple tasks (typo fixes, dependency bumps) to lightweight processing without full agentic loops
Use Container Apps Consumption plan with scale-to-zero for non-business-hours — agent instances only running when tasks are queued, eliminating idle compute costs overnight and weekends
